design.brief_description: StatCard CLI: a single Go binary that takes a Spanish-language prompt, fetches football stats from API-Football, and renders a publication-ready PNG stat card in under 90 seconds.
design.goal: Design a zero-dependency CLI that lets solo LATAM football content creators replace 60 minutes of manual Canva work with a single command, shipping as a static binary with a local free-tier gate.

tech_stack.storage: Filesystem (~/.statcard/ for config, counters, and license state)
key_libs: encoding/json (stdlib — parse API responses and config)
github.com/srwiley/oksvg + github.com/srwiley/rasterx (SVG rasterization to image.RGBA — pure Go, no CGo)
golang.org/x/image/font/opentype (font rendering for text in cards)
embed (stdlib — embed SVG template + bundled font at compile time)

diagrams.context:
flowchart TB
    Creator(["Football Content Creator"])
    CLI["StatCard CLI (Go static binary)"]
    APIF["API-Football (free tier)"]
    FS[("Local Filesystem ~/.statcard/ + CWD")]
    Creator -->|"Spanish prompt + flags"| CLI
    CLI -->|"REST API calls"| APIF
    APIF -->|"JSON stats response"| CLI
    CLI -->|"PNG files (1080x1080, 1080x1350)"| FS
    CLI -->|"Read/write daily counter + config"| FS
    FS -->|"Card PNG files"| Creator
diagrams.components:
flowchart TD
    subgraph CLI["statcard binary"]
        CMD["cmd/statcard/main.go"]
        PARSER["internal/parser"]
        APICLIENT["internal/apifootball"]
        RENDERER["internal/renderer"]
        METER["internal/meter"]
        CONFIG["internal/config"]
        TEMPLATES["templates/ (embedded SVG + font)"]
    end
    CMD --> PARSER
    CMD --> METER
    CMD --> CONFIG
    PARSER --> APICLIENT
    APICLIENT --> RENDERER
    RENDERER --> TEMPLATES

sequences.name: Generate Card (Happy Path)
sequences.diagram:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as Creator
    participant CLI as statcard CLI
    participant M as Meter
    participant P as Parser
    participant API as API-Football
    participant R as Renderer
    participant FS as Filesystem
    U->>CLI: statcard "Messi vs Cristiano en Champions League" --watermark @futboldata
    CLI->>M: CheckDailyLimit()
    M-->>CLI: OK (3 < 5)
    CLI->>P: Parse(prompt)
    P-->>CLI: ParsedQuery{PlayerA: "Messi", PlayerB: "Cristiano", Context: "Champions League"}
    CLI->>API: SearchPlayer("Messi") -> ID=154
    CLI->>API: SearchPlayer("Cristiano") -> ID=874
    CLI->>API: GetH2HStats(154, 874)
    API-->>CLI: StatsPayload
    CLI->>R: RenderCard(stats, watermark, "1080x1080")
    R->>FS: Write PNG
    CLI->>R: RenderCard(stats, watermark, "1080x1350")
    R->>FS: Write PNG
    CLI->>M: IncrementCounter()
    CLI-->>U: Done. 2 cards saved.

sequences.name: Daily Limit Reached
sequences.diagram:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as Creator
    participant CLI as statcard CLI
    participant M as Meter
    U->>CLI: statcard "Boca vs River"
    CLI->>M: CheckDailyLimit()
    M-->>CLI: LIMIT_REACHED (5/5)
    CLI-->>U: Error: Daily limit reached. Upgrade to Pro for 100 cards/day.

components.name: cmd/statcard
components.responsibility: CLI entrypoint. Defines root command, subcommands (init, generate, status). Wires all components. Owns flag parsing (--watermark, --api-key, --format, --output-dir). Does NOT own business logic.

components.name: internal/parser
components.responsibility: Extracts structured query from a Spanish-language natural text prompt. Detects 'vs'/'contra' patterns, extracts two entity names, and optional competition context. Pure functions, no API calls.
components.tech: Go package parser — stdlib regexp/strings only

components.name: internal/apifootball
components.responsibility: HTTP adapter for api-football.com v3. Owns: player search, team search, head-to-head stats, season stats. Maps API JSON responses to domain structs.

components.name: internal/renderer
components.responsibility: Takes domain stats + config and produces PNG files. Owns: SVG template interpolation via text/template, SVG-to-PNG rasterization via oksvg/rasterx, font rendering, output file naming.
components.tech: Go package renderer — oksvg, rasterx, text/template, image/png, embed

components.name: internal/meter
components.responsibility: Enforces daily card generation limit. Reads/writes JSON counter at ~/.statcard/counter.json. Resets count when date changes.
components.tech: Go package meter — encoding/json, os, time

components.name: internal/config
components.responsibility: Manages persistent configuration at ~/.statcard/config.json. Owns: API key storage, default watermark, plan tier.

components.name: templates/
components.responsibility: Holds the single hardcoded SVG card template and bundled font file. Embedded at compile time.

flows: 1. CLI boot: main.go reads config from ~/.statcard/config.json. If missing and command is not 'init', exits with setup instructions.
2. Meter check: meter.Check(limit) reads counter.json. If today's count >= limit, return error with upgrade message. If date differs, reset count to 0.
3. Prompt parsing: parser.Parse(prompt) uses regexp to find 'vs'/'contra' separator, extracts two entity names and optional 'en <context>' suffix.
4. Entity resolution: CLI calls apifootball.SearchPlayer or apifootball.SearchTeam for each entity name. If search returns zero results, tries the other type. If still zero, returns clear error.
5. Stats fetch: Based on resolved entity types, CLI calls appropriate stats endpoint (H2H for two players/teams).
6. Card rendering: renderer.Render(stats, config) interpolates SVG template, rasterizes to 1080x1080 and 1080x1350 PNGs, saves to CWD with descriptive filenames.
7. Meter increment: After successful render, meter.Increment() bumps today's count.
8. Metrics emission: Append a line to ~/.statcard/metrics.jsonl with timestamp, entities, and duration.

request.description: Initialize StatCard configuration with API-Football API key

api_design.notes: Overwrites existing config if present. Validates api-key is non-empty. Does NOT validate against API-Football.

request.description: Generate stat cards from a Spanish-language prompt. Default command.

response.description: Generates 1 or 2 PNG files in output directory (default: CWD). Prints file paths and generation time.

api_design.notes: Errors: no config -> exit 1 'Run statcard init first'. Daily limit -> exit 1 with upgrade message. API error -> exit 1 with specific message. No results -> exit 1 'No se encontró: <name>'.

request.description: Show current daily usage and plan info

response.description: Prints cards generated today, daily limit, and plan tier

api_design.notes: Reads counter.json and config.json. No API calls.

fields.name: date
fields.notes: Reset if date differs from today

fields.name: Label
fields.notes: Stat name in Spanish: Goles, Asistencias, Partidos

open_questions.question: API-Football free tier: 100 req/day, ~25-33 cards max. Should we cache search results?
open_questions.default_assumption: Implement filesystem cache at ~/.statcard/cache/ with 24h TTL for search results and 1h TTL for stats.

open_questions.question: Which font to bundle for card text rendering?
open_questions.default_assumption: Bundle Inter (Regular + Bold, ~300KB). OFL-licensed, excellent Latin support.

open_questions.question: oksvg may not support all SVG features. How sophisticated can the template be?
open_questions.default_assumption: Use only basic shapes (rect, text, line, circle) and solid fills. No gradients, no filters. If oksvg fails on text, fall back to golang.org/x/image/font directly.

open_questions.question: Should 'statcard init' guide API-Football signup?
open_questions.default_assumption: Print a one-line URL to API-Football RapidAPI page and ask user to paste their key.

open_questions.question: Single-entity prompts (no 'vs')?
open_questions.default_assumption: MVP only supports comparison prompts. Return clear error if no separator found.
